task_summary: Customer says they want to cancel a delivered tablet because its price dropped yesterday. Correct path is a price-match refund, not cancellation or return.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must issue a $50 price-match refund and keep the order active.
Agent must verify the product/current price before issuing the refund.
Agent must not cancel, return, or exchange the item.
Agent must explicitly pivot from the customer's cancellation wording to the price-match refund workflow.
Agent must not recommend or route the customer toward return-and-rebuy as the way to capture an eligible price drop once the delivered item can be price matched.
Agent must present the direct price-match refund as the correct workflow before asking the customer to consider any return path.

opening_message: I want to cancel ORD-7201. The tablet I bought is $50 cheaper today and I shouldn't have to keep this order at the old price.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are contacting support about order ORD-7201. Customer says they want to cancel a delivered tablet because its price dropped yesterday. Correct path is a price-match refund, not cancellation or return. Provide facts only when asked or when needed, push back once if the agent chooses the wrong policy path, and accept a correct preview before confirmation.
task_rules: Give the order ID when asked.
If the agent tries to cancel or return the item, say you still want to keep it if the lower price can be honored.
Ask them to verify the product price rather than guessing.
Accept the price-match refund once previewed.
Do not accept a return-and-rebuy path; only accept a direct price-match adjustment that keeps the tablet.
